methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kraken Robotics is a public-stage company operating in the Defense & Security sector. The comparable universe consists of 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private competitors, including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries. This represents a total of 10 identifiable comparables across the sector.

The peer-median method was deemed inapplicable in this analysis. Despite identifying 24 same-sector peers in Defense & Security, the application of stage-specific, adjacency-based, and valuation-tier filters yielded fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for statistical reliability. A median derived from all-sector Defense & Security peers would introduce material cross-segment distortion and obscure meaningful valuation signals specific to Kraken's business model and market position.

Consequently, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range at this time. The primary constraint is the absence of a sufficiently dense peer cohort meeting narrow comparability criteria. To advance a defensible valuation, additional granularity would be required on the financial profiles, growth trajectories, and recent transaction multiples of the identified private peers—particularly the top five—or expanded stage-specific public comps. The key uncertainty remains whether Kraken's revenue scale, margin profile, and customer concentration align more closely with the defense-focused public set or the earlier-stage private pool.
